summary: Iter-7 embeddable widget testing. Backend: 18/25 tests pass; 6 critical failures all from a SINGLE root cause — the /embed/{slug} FastAPI route is mounted on `app` (NOT under /api), so the platform's Kubernetes ingress routes /embed/* to the React frontend (port 3000) instead of the FastAPI backend (port 8001). curl https://<preview>/embed/<slug> returns the React index.html, NOT the FastAPI iframe HTML. The backend code itself is correct — `curl http://localhost:8001/embed/<slug>` returns proper HTML with TokenForge savings title, ALLOWALL X-Frame-Options, frame-ancestors CSP, light/dark theme palette, 404 fallback, and 100%-capped avg_pct (all verified via internal-localhost tests). Request author flagged this exact possibility. FIX: move route to /api/embed/{slug} and update Share.jsx + Docs.jsx + the widget.js loader (which builds origin + '/embed/' + slug) accordingly. /api/widget.js itself works correctly (200, application/javascript, ACAO:*, IIFE with data-tf-slug + currentScript). avg_compression_pct cap works (JSON API returns 100.0 even when raw saved/original >1). Frontend Share page UI is fully wired: share-embed-btn toggles embed-snippet-panel, snippet correctly contains /api/widget.js + slug + theme attr, embed-theme-dark/light swaps both snippet attr AND iframe src, iframe defaults to theme=dark. The live preview iframe loads — but shows the React SPA (TokenForge landing) instead of the savings widget (same ingress bug). Copy button fires but in the headless/iframe context navigator.clipboard.writeText rejects → app shows fallback toast 'Copy failed — select & copy manually'. All prior critical flows (auth, optimize, billing monthly+annual, savings PDF, dashboard, share, admin RBAC) still 100% green.

critical.issue: Route unreachable via public preview URL — ingress sends non-/api paths to React frontend, so https://<host>/embed/<slug> returns the React index.html, not the FastAPI widget HTML. Backend code is correct (verified via http://localhost:8001/embed/<slug>). Move route to /api/embed/{slug} OR add an ingress exception for /embed/*. Author of the request explicitly said: 'if so, I will move it'.

minor.issue: HEAD returns Content-Type: application/json (FastAPI auto-HEAD doesn't preserve the GET response's media_type). GET works correctly (application/javascript). Hosts only use <script src> which issues GET so this is cosmetic, but worth noting.

integration_issues.flow: Share page → Embed widget preview iframe
integration_issues.issue: embed-preview-iframe src is correct (BACKEND_URL/embed/<slug>?theme=dark) but renders the React SPA instead of the savings widget because the same /embed ingress-routing bug. Same fix in backend will resolve this — just update Share.jsx, Docs.jsx, and the widget.js loader to use /api/embed/.

navigator.clipboard.writeText rejected in cross-origin/iframe context, toast 'Copy failed — select & copy manually' is shown (graceful fallback works). In a real top-level browser context with HTTPS this works fine; only a concern if Share is ever embedded itself.

action_items: CRITICAL: Move /embed/{slug} from `app.get('/embed/{slug}')` to `@api.get('/embed/{slug}')` so its public URL becomes /api/embed/<slug> and reaches FastAPI through the ingress. Then update: (a) Share.jsx line 186 iframe src to `${BACKEND_URL}/api/embed/${slug}?theme=${theme}`, (b) the JS loader string in widget_loader() to build `origin + '/api/embed/' + slug + '?theme=' + theme`, (c) Docs.jsx embed section examples, (d) any 404 fallback link.
Re-run iter7 widget tests after the fix; the 6 failing TestEmbedPagePublic tests will all pass (backend code itself is already correct — confirmed via localhost-direct tests).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1076 — `@app.get('/embed/{slug}')` is the root cause of the public-URL miss. Must live under the `api` router (i.e. `@api.get('/embed/{slug}')`). Backend internal correctness is otherwise solid: ALLOWALL XFO + frame-ancestors CSP + 404 HTML fallback (not JSON detail) + light/dark palette swap + avg_pct cap all confirmed.

server.py:998-1000 — avg_pct cap `min(100.0, round(saved/original*100, 2))` correctly bounds the displayed %. Verified: a slug whose raw saved/original==2.0 (cache hits crediting completion tokens) now displays 100.0% instead of 200%.

server.py:1014 widget_loader uses `new URL(s.src).origin` then appends `/embed/<slug>` — after the route move, this MUST be updated to `origin + '/api/embed/' + ...` or hosts that paste the script will iframe the React SPA instead of the widget.

server.py:1051 iframe sandbox 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-popups' triggers a browser warning (these two flags together let it escape sandboxing). Acceptable for first-party widget since we control /embed, but consider dropping allow-same-origin (postMessage works without it) for stricter posture.
Share.jsx:186 hardcodes BACKEND_URL/embed/... → same path-move required in frontend after backend route relocation.
Share.jsx clipboard fallback toast already exists ('Copy failed — select & copy manually'), so no further action needed beyond optionally adding execCommand('copy') fallback for older browsers.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_tokenforge_iter7_widget.py (new — 25 tests covering widget.js, /embed public, /embed internal-localhost, avg_pct cap, full regression smoke)

success_rate.backend: 72% (18/25 pass). All 6 TestEmbedPagePublic failures share ONE root cause = ingress routing. Backend code is correct (proven by TestEmbedPageInternal tests against localhost:8001). 1 internal-localhost test failed only because of an over-strict assertion in my test (#0A0A0A appears as the text color in light theme as well as fonts.googleapis.com unrelated reference) — not a code bug. Real backend coverage including widget.js, JSON avg_pct cap, and full regression: 100%.
success_rate.frontend: 90% (Share page UI fully wired and correct — toggle, snippet, theme switch all working. Live-preview iframe correctly built but renders the wrong content due to the backend ingress bug, not a frontend bug.)

test_credentials: admin@tokenforge.io / ForgeAdmin!2026 (admin); alreadyherellc@gmail.com / ForgeQA!2026 (QA). Admin slug created: Hq2gSRLFuypZ (used for both backend and frontend tests).
seed_data_creation: Reused admin share slug (Hq2gSRLFuypZ — already existed from prior iterations). Ephemeral TEST_iter7_*@example.com throwaway users for RBAC + register-then-me regression tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_tokenforge_iter7_widget.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iter7_widget.xml. After main agent moves /embed to /api/embed, update test BASE references: replace `f"{BASE}/embed/"` with `f"{API}/embed/"` in TestEmbedPagePublic (3 places). TestEmbedPageInternal still hits localhost:8001/embed unless main agent removes the app-level route entirely — if they do, switch internal tests to localhost:8001/api/embed too. The one false-positive (test_embed_internal_light_palette) can be deleted or loosened — its #0A0A0A check is over-strict; light theme legitimately uses #0A0A0A for text color. The Share.jsx iframe src will also need to become `${BACKEND_URL}/api/embed/${slug}?theme=...` after the move; verify in browser that the iframe now shows the savings widget instead of the React SPA.
rca_of_the_issue: Reproduction: `curl -i https://market-launch-43.preview.emergentagent.com/embed/Hq2gSRLFuypZ` returns HTTP 200 with the React index.html (you can see the <meta description='TokenForge — Cut your LLM costs by 40-80%'> from the SPA, not the FastAPI <title>TokenForge savings — TokenForge Admin</title>). In contrast `curl http://localhost:8001/embed/Hq2gSRLFuypZ` returns the correct FastAPI HTML with all expected headers (X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL, Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors *). Root cause: per /app environment notes, the Kubernetes ingress only routes paths starting with /api to port 8001; everything else goes to port 3000 (React). `@app.get('/embed/{slug}')` is mounted on the FastAPI root app outside the `api` router prefix, so the ingress never routes /embed/* to it. Mitigation: change decorator from `@app.get("/embed/{slug}")` to `@api.get("/embed/{slug}")` (making the public path /api/embed/<slug>), then update the widget.js loader string and Share.jsx iframe src + Docs.jsx examples to use the /api/embed/ path. Verified backend logic itself is bug-free via direct localhost calls.
